Tag Name: Auto Direct To Next Entry On Submit
Description: Causes the submit of each entry to redirect the user to the next entry.
Tag Help:
Plugins
DirectiveToEntryOnValidation [ENotebook Entry: Validation] (MIDDLE)
This is a Notebook Entry Option tag that causes the submit of an entry to redirect the user to the 'next' entry. There are three options:
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO NEXT ENTRY DEPENDENT ON THIS ONE)
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO NEXT ENTRY IN LINEAR SEQUENCE)
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO FIRST ENTRY IN NEXT TAB)
TO NEXT ENTRY DEPENDENT ON THIS ONE will check each entry for dependent entries, and redirect to the next one.
TO NEXT ENTRY IN LINEAR SEQUENCE will direct the user to the entry immediately below the entry being submitted.
TO FIRST ENTRY IN NEXT TAB will direct the user to the first entry on the next tab following the current entry's tab.
If there is not an available entry to direct to, this tag will do nothing.
E-Notebook Entry Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook Entry option keys.
Notebook Entry Key: AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT
Permutations
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO NEXT ENTRY DEPENDENT ON THIS ONE)
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO NEXT ENTRY IN LINEAR SEQUENCE)
AUTO DIRECT TO NEXT ENTRY ON SUBMIT (TO FIRST ENTRY IN NEXT TAB)

Tag Name: Auto Initialize and Submit Next Entry
Description: This is an entry option that causes the initiation and optional validation of the 'next' entry
Tag Help:
Plugins
AutoInitializeValidateNextEntryValidationPlugin [ENotebook Entry: Validation] (LAST + 5)
This is an entry option that causes the 'next' entry in the notebook to be initialized and optionally validated. The next entry is determined by
The subsequent entry in the notebook from the current entry
Based on the entry option value where the value is the name of the entry�
Ex. In a notebook where the option is placed on the Aliquot Plate GUI entry and the next entry is the "Aliquoting Plate Details" entry
AUTO INITIALIZE NEXT ENTRY with no option value would initialize the Aliquoting Plate Details table task
AUTO INITIALIZE AND VALIDATE NEXT ENTRY with no option value would initialize and validate the "Aliquoting Plate Details" entry
AUTO INITIALIZE NEXT ENTRY with value "Aliquoting Plate Details" will initialize the entry named "Aliquoting Plate Details"

Description: Completes a notebook experiment with various options to enhance the user experience in an NGS lab
Tag Help:
Plugins
Overview
The complete workflow button will give the user one button to manage the completion of a notebook. This button will provide consistency across ELN templates throughout a process while allowing flexibility for admins to dictate whether all entries need to be completed prior to notebook completion or if e-signatures are required.
Description
The SHOW COMPLETE WORKFLOW BUTTON tag must be present in the Notebook Options for this button to appear.
All option values are not required. If no option values are given, the following defaults will apply:
E-Signatures are required
All entries must be validated prior to completing the workflow
Allows anyone with 'write' access (Author or Owner) to complete the workflow
Values/Modifiers
Modifiers give the administrator a means to adjust the Complete Workflow button to fit the needs of the lab without requiring consulting work. The following option values are available:
ESIGN NOT REQUIRED
By default, esign is required by any user attempting to complete a workflow. If present, users with proper permissions (Authors and Owners) are able to complete the workflow immediately without providing login credentials.
NO COMMENT REQUIRED FOR ESIGN
Prevents the comment box from appearing during e-sign allowing for only username/password to continue. Only applicable if 'ESIGN NOT REQUIRED' is not also present.
RESTRICT TO OWNER
The generic E-Sign button for the ELN restricts completion to only the Owner. By default, the Complete Workflow button allows anyone with Write access to be able to complete workflows. However, this can be overridden to function similarly to the E-Sign button and require the Owner to e-sign.
ALL ENTRY VALIDATIONS NOT REQUIRED
By default all entries will be required to be validated before, but with this tag you will be able to complete the workflow without validating all entries.
Experiment Entry Tags
WORKFLOW COMPLETED ON VALIDATION
If at least one experiment entry contains this tag, it is required to be validated prior to the workflow being completed. Any other entry in the experiment, other than those with the REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION, will not impact the completion of the workflow whether they are validated or not.
REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION
�Any experiment entry with this tag must be validated prior to the completion of the workflow.
E-Notebook Options

NOTE:�This tag will be overridden by the WORKFLOW COMPLETED ON VALIDATION and REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION entry level tags. If those tags are present on any Experiment Entry, those corresponding entries will require validation prior to completing the workflow.

Description: Track reagents and other consumables throughout workflow.
Tag Help:
Plugins
DefaultMaterialsManagementPlugin
This task option key performs a number of actions relating to Materials Management in general. This tag is expected to appear in the task tag section to enable multiple field specific actions, which also require field tags. In particular:
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING Consumable Type -->
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE Consumable Type -->
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING MAP TO fieldName -->
<!-- CONSUMABLE PARTNUMBER Consumable Type -->
<!-- CONSUMABLE EXPIRATION Consumable Type -->
<!-- USE HIDDEN Some Value -->
<!-- KIT CONSUMABLE TRACKING Kit Name -->
Task Entry
Upon task entry, this tag will perform the following:
If the task contains a EDIT PROTOCOLS BUTTON task option key (tag) then a button named "Sample Level" will be displayed in the task form and table toolbars. This button will allow a user to edit consumable information associated with individual samples.
If the task contains a SCAN KIT task option key (tag) then a button named "Scan Kit" will appear in the task form and table toolbars. This button will allow a kit lot number / barcode to be scanned which will automatically fill in associated lot numbers / barcodes on the displayed task.
If either <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING Consumable Type --> or <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE Consumable Type --> tags are found on the fields displayed in the current task and field tag <!-- DEFAULT TO LAST USED CONSUMABLE --> is not present, the fields will get automatically populated by either a lot number or a barcode associated with a proper Consumable Item as indicated in the tag. The Consumable Item is selected by referencing following parameters:
If the configurations are set to enforce expiration dates then a consumable item with the closest upcoming expiration date will be selected.
If the configurations are set to track quantity then consumable items with a quantity equal to 0 or null will not be taken into consideration.
Note: Configuration settings are found on a data record called Exemplar Configuration which is normally accessed through the Exemplar Configurations workflow.
If the�<!-- DEFAULT TO LAST USED CONSUMABLE --> field tag is found, then the system will default to the last used consumable lot number.
Task Submit
Upon task submission, this tag will perform the following:
All fields marked with <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING Consumable Type --> or <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE Consumable Type --> will be evaluated. If any of the Text or Selection fields with filled in values are found, the system will attempt to match the values with proper Consumable Items as indicated in the field tags.
If a consumable item is not found:
If configuration settings allow new consumable item creation from within a workflow, then a user will be prompted with a new Consumable Item creation dialog. This dialog display varies depending on description availability of a related Consumable record.
If configuration settings do not allow new Consumable Item creation from within a workflow, then an error will be displayed to the user.
Note: Addition of fields with tags such as <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING MAP TO fieldName -->, <!-- CONSUMABLE PARTNUMBER Consumable Type --> and <!-- CONSUMABLE EXPIRATION Consumable Type --> will further pinpoint the system to a proper Consumable Item. Furthermore, it is possible to include both <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING Consumable Type --> and <!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE Consumable Type --> tags on the same form, pointing to the same Consumable Item.
If a Consumable Item is found:
Consumable Items associated with their corresponding fields will get added to the current experiment.
If configuration settings are set to track quantity and if a numeric field (Long, Double) marked with a similar tag is found, then a value indicated in the field will get subtracted from the associated Consumable Item. This will in turn update the total available quantity on the parent Consumable record. If the quantity falls below 0, then a message will be displayed to the user.
If configuration settings are set to track expiration dates and the found Consumable Item is deemed expired, then an error will be presented to the user detailing the situation.
A Consumable Usage Lot record will be created to record current actions. This record is then used to revert changes if a user decided to reenter the task, and change Consumable related values.
TASK OPTION VALUES
"EDIT PROTOCOLS BUTTON" task option value for CONSUMABLE TRACKING indicates that the current task contains fields that may be associated with consumable tracking using multiple samples. Since it is possible to use multiple lot numbers in the processing of all selected samples, we define a way to partner specific items with their corresponding samples. Implementation of this feature is performed via a popup table, which allows a user to enter lot numbers used for particular samples.
FIELD TAGS
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING Consumable Type -->
This is a field tag indicating that the field is associated with consumable tracking. Following the "<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING part, the tag must indicate the Consumable Type as indicated in the ConsumableType field on the Consumable data record of interest. If the field is set to support more than one Consumable Type, then delimit Consumable Types with a pipe (|).
Example:
Field Tag	Description
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING PhiX Library -->	Use the current field as a place holder for recording a lot number associated with the used PhiX Library reagent.
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING DNA Extraction Kit | RNA Extraction Kit -->	Use the current field as a place holder for recording a lot number associated with the used DNA Extraction Kit or RNA Extraction Kit. A user will be presented with a choice for which kit this lot number should be associated with.
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE Consumable Type -->
This tag indicates that the "Consumable Type" indicated in this tag is to be tracked by individual bottle / container rather than a lot number. The value entered into this field is expected to be the barcode for the Consumable Item.
The barcode entered should form a unique combination with a lot number and a part number within the system.
Example:
Field Tag	Description
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE PhiX Library -->	Use the current field as a place holder for recording a barcode associated with the used PhiX Library reagent.
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING USE BARCODE DNA Extraction Kit | RNA Extraction Kit -->	Use the current field as a place holder for recording a barcode associated with the used DNA Extraction Kit or RNA Extraction Kit. A user will be presented with a choice for which kit this barcode should be associated with.
<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING MAP TO fieldName -->
When the consumable type is not a statically defined value, it may be dynamically specified in a field either via a direct user interface or through a plugin. This is supported via a consumable tracking field tag that points to another field used in defining this Consumable Type value. Implementation of this is such that if field1 contains a consumable type supplied by the user, and field2 is used in recording the lot number or barcode for the consumable defined in field1, then the field tag presented on field2 will point the system to field1 in obtaining the consumable type value. Likewise, the same tag will need to be present on the quantity field if the used quantity is to be recorded.
Example:
The following example points lot number and quantity fields to use a separate field for a consumable type value.
Field Name	Field Tag	Value	Result
ConsumableTypeField	N/A	PhiX Library	The consumable type will be evaluated to a "PhiX Library" with a lot number of "1234" out of which a value of 30.23 will be deducted.
LotNumberField	<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING MAP TO ConsumableTypeField -->	1234
QtyField	<!-- CONSUMABLE TRACKING MAP TO ConsumableTypeField -->	30.23
<!-- CONSUMABLE PARTNUMBER Consumable Type -->
This tag allows specification of a part number within a task form. If a part number is entered and the consumable item does not exist, then the displayed form will automatically include the entered part number. If the consumable is found with a matching part number, then that consumable will be used as a parent record of the newly created consumable item.

So to support it, a plugin containing this custom code can be specified here.
To make this work:
Create a plugin class that would show up in the plugin descriptor result of serverPluginManager.getDescriptorList()
(Optional) In the shouldRun() method, specify when this should be executed
If not specified, then it would run every time, which may not actually be a big problem since it would only run when this tag is present in the entry options
In the overridden run() method, add the custom logic that should happen when the activeStep is being unlocked

Description: These are a collection of plugins which supports E-signature features.
Tag Help:
Plugins
LockEntryValidation [ENotebook Entry: Validation] (MIDDLE)
LockNotebook [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
UnlockEntry [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
UnlockNotebook [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
ViewNotebookEntryESignature [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
ViewNotebookESignature [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
LockEntryToolbar [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
E-Signature for Electronic Notebook
Sapio ELN allows users to e-sign notebooks and individual entries in notebooks and work them throughout an approval process. The plugins are tag driven so the button would only appear on buttons where e-signature makes sense. It is possible to turn on and off the features individually. For example, some notebooks may not allow ELN admins to unlock, some notebooks always requires approval, some notebooks may require approvals for entries but not the notebook. This documentation describes how to use all of these options.
Any user who have read access to a notebook may put their signature on a notebook with signing enabled for the user role. However, the effect of the signature depends on the user role in the notebook. Owner�will first sign with the notebook unlocked, then other users would sign when the notebook is locked. Once the notebooks are locked, authors may no longer edit. Depending on configurations, all other authors may be required to sign at this point. Afterwards, approvers, reviewers, and witnesses may sign as long as their signatures are required and the notebook is in the state awaiting for approval.
The configurations may be defined by either Exemplar ELN Configurations, or notebook and entry options on templates. The template options takes priority over settings defined in Exemplar ELN Configurations.
Notebooks may require some or all entries to be completed before anyone can continue with E-Sign process. This is customizable through additional experiment and entry keys. See key descriptions for details.
The Approval Process
(Owner Starts E-Sign Process) -> (Optional: all other authors signs) -> (Optional: One Witness) -> (Optional: One Reviewer) -> (Optional: One Approver)
These are steps a notebook/entry would go through for a typical approval process:
The notebook/entry has e-sign signature enabled.
The author finish all changes to the notebook finishes assigning user roles. Then either one author or the owner signs off the notebook. At this point, all user roles are locked in. This means no one can change the author/approver/reviewer/witness list. During the sign-off, the author enters an approval due date. The due date is optional and will appear in log and notifications if specified.
Everyone who is not the author or owner whose signatures are required by the tag option now signs off. The order is Witness->Reviewer->Approver. Once a user of that role signs off, E-sign will progress to the next role and no other users in the same role may change the verdict.
During signing, any user may enter a verdict. Even if another user in the same role had previously accepted the notebook or entry.
If the verdict is Accepted
If this is the last signature required, then the status will be changed to Completed - Approved.
If it still needs more signatures, then the status will still be Locked - Awaiting Approval. But a notification will still be sent to the authors and owners.
If the verdict is Rejected, the notebook is marked as Rejected - Final, and cannot be edited further.
If the verdict is Changes Required, the notebook is marked as Unlocked - Changes Required. Go back to step 2.
A notification will be sent to every author and owner of the notebook.
Owners and Authors
Only owner may start E-sign process. Other authors may not sign until owner signs first.
If owner and author are both specified in options,the owner must initiate the E-sign process. Afterwards,�all authors�must complete e-sign before approver/reviewer/witnesses are notified for their E-signature. If some author want to make changes the author can unlock the notebook.
If owner is specified in options, but author is not, then only the owner may E-sign.Other authors do not participate in this E-sign process.
E-Sign Notebooks Without Approval
Notebooks which are not eligible for the approval process may be signed by authors to lock the notebook and set the notebook status to Completed. After the notebook is locked, authors are no longer allowed to edit the notebook.
This is enabled by the tag option "Owner", which specifies only owner signatures are required, or not having a tag option at all and also leave E-Signature fields blank in exemplar configurations.
ELN Admin Unlock
An ELN admin may always unlock any notebook or any notebook entry as long as it makes sense. They will first acquire access to the notebook, then the unlock button will appear for them to unlock the notebook or notebook entry.
An ELN admin may unlock the notebook if and only if the status of the notebook is Locked(Waiting for Approval/Rejected Final) or Completed(Completed or Completed with Approval).
An ELN admin may unlock an entry in notebook if and only if all of the followings are true:
The notebook is currently unlocked. If the notebook is locked, the admin needs to first unlock the notebook before unlocking any entries in the notebook.
The status of the entry is Locked(Waiting for Approval/Rejected Final) or Completed(Completed or Completed with Approval).
Once the ELN admin performs any of these actions, all authors of the notebook will be notified about the unlock action via both broadcast message and email. This notification cannot be suppressed by tag options.
The admin unlock is a special e-sign action which also requires the admin to re-authenticate.
Only the ELN Admin, owner of the experiment, and the original creator of the entry may unlock the entry while the entry is locked during E-sign process.
Authentication Form
The authentication form will be displayed for each E-sign action, including locking and unlocking. The form may provide additional fields to fill. Author E-sign requires authors to fill in a due date, Approver E-Sign requires approvers to fill in a verdict.
All e-signatures requires the username of the e-authentication form to match the username of the user logged in. It also requires the password to match the password of the current user. The comment is optional and will be shown in the view e-sign history.

Description: This tag is used to initiate process tracking for a workflow on submit. Samples, plates, or plate wells can be attached to the task. The plugin will update all of their statuses, their assigned processes, and any other affected samples' or plate wells' statuses too.
Tag Help:
Plugins
EnterWorkflowTracking [Task: Submit] (LATE)
"ENTER WORKFLOW" or "ENTER WORKFLOW WITH PROTOCOLS" task option key is used to initiate process tracking for a workflow on submit. Samples, plates, or plate wells can be attached to the task. The plugin will update all of their statuses, their assigned processes, and any other affected samples' or plate wells' statuses too.
Once that has all been completed, protocols will be attached as children of the samples or plate wells. If "WITH PROTOCOLS" is included in the key, then the protocols will be retrieved from earlier in the workflow and attached to the samples. If it is not present, then the protocols will be created at this point. Lastly, if there is a value specified for this task option, then that will be used as the new sample type for these samples or plate wells.
"SKIP ASSIGNED PROCESS REMOVAL" is a task option value that is used in conjunction with the task option key ENTER WORKFLOW. If this task option value is present with the "ENTER WORKFLOW" task option key, then the assigned process records that are not ready for this current workflow will not be removed from the samples. This is intended to be used for workflows that are not necessarily in the current process but are still accessible through the work queue with the use of the Assign to Process functionality. With this the workflows can be more modular in processes and can be pulled in when needed.
Runs LATE on submit.

Description: This tag allows the user to specify to return a sample to a previous reprocessing point, or to fail samples. Failed Samples will have the assigned processes for that workflow changed to \"Failed -\" + current workflow name. The rest of its assigned processes for that sample will be adjusted accordingly. This task tag is used for steps typically done after a QC occurs or during technician/manager inspection of the data. It is fully compatible with the Override Button plugin located in QC.
Tag Help:
Plugins
HandleReprocessing [Task: Submit] (LAST)
RemoveFailedSamplesOnEntry [Task: Entry] (MIDDLE)
HandleReprocessingUtils [Task: Submit] (LAST)
�HANDLE REPROCESSING (SampleId|SampleRecordId)
Category: Process Tracking
The Handle Reprocessing plugin allows the user to specify to return a sample to a previous reprocessing point, or to fail samples. Failed Samples will have the assigned processes for that workflow changed to "Failed -" + current workflow name. The rest of its assigned processes for that sample will be adjusted accordingly. This task tag is used for steps typically done after a QC occurs or during technician/manager inspection of the data. It is fully compatible with the Override Button plugin located in QC (@see OVERRIDE_BUTTON).
@order Last @invocation TaskSubmit @description In order to use this task plugin, there are two major requirements: Tag setup and dataType setup.
Tag Requirements:
required Task Tag Key:
"HANDLE REPROCESSING" tag comes in two choices:
Key 1: "HANDLE REPROCESSING SampleId"
Key 2: "HANDLE REPROCESSING SampleRecordId"
Use only one of them for the Task Key.
Using SampleId means the reprocessing plugin will look on the dataType specified by the task definition for the SampleId. It will then query the plateWells and Samples based on those SampleIds.
This is primarily used when the dataType being used already contains the sampleId, such as Sample, PlateWell, AggregateDatum, or any other dataType containing SampleId.
Using SampleRecordId means the reprocessing plugin will look on the dataType specified by the task definition for the SampleRecordId. It will then query plateWells and samples for those RecordIds.
This is typically used in post processing steps where there may no samples in the workflow at all.
The purpose of specifying SampleId or SampleRecordId is to find the associated sample for the status being shown to the user. The status's displayed on the records attached to the task will be used to determine the status of the associated sample for reprocessing.
For example: An AggregateDatum displayed to the user contains a sampleId of "ABC123". It has a status of "Reprocess". The plugin backing this tag will find sample with sampleId = "ABC123", and start reprocessing for it.
optional Task Tag Values: None or more of these may be present in the key for this task tag.
"REQUIRED": All samples found by the handle reprocessing plugin must have a non empty status: They should have been run through QC at least once or otherwise have a status applied to them.
"REMOVE FAILURES": The task will remove the failed samples and protocols from further attachment points in the workflow. In the event that no samples remain, the workflow should terminate. Terminating the workflow is outside the scope of this plugin.
"WORKFLOW COMPLETE IF ALL FAILED": When all records are marked for reprocessing or removed from the workflow then there is no need to continue with said workflow in most cases. This task option value automatically marks all tasks complete and exits the workflow only if there are no Samples, Plates, or PlateWells found attached to the task with a process status to continue.
"DATA TYPE NAME [DnaExtractionProtocol]": Specify the name of the data type that should be evaluated. This is useful when a task could have multiple output data types, and in cases when the displayed values are via a temporary data collection task and backed by attached records. An example would be: A task is displaying a compilation of values from the DNA Extraction Protocol record and DNA Extraction Experiment record. The compilation had to be done via a temporary data collection task, where the reprocessing status field is on the protocol. Specifying that the system should work with a data type of DNA Extraction Protocol will have the system retrieve protocol records attached to the task and continue execution as if they were displayed natively.
DataType Requirements:
required Field Tag:
"<!-- HANDLE REPROCESSING -->" field tag must be present on a selection or pick list field that may have values containing the (case insensitive) strings: "reprocess", "retest", "re-test", "failed". It does not need to have all of them, just the ones the user needs to pick from.
Any status that does not contain one of those statuses is considered to be passing.
Failed is a lower ranking status than reprocessing, so a value of "Failed->Reprocess" will end up being reprocessed.
required String Field:
"SampleId" or Long "SampleRecordId". The field should be 1 per sample record and contain the appropriate field ("SampleId" and "RecordId") mapped to it.

HANDLE REPROCESSING SampleRecordId

Tag Name: Lock All Related Entries On Submit
Description: This will lock all related entries on submit of this entry.
Tag Help:
Plugins
LockRelatedEntriesOnSubmit [ENotebook Entry: Validation] (MIDDLE)
This tag is an experiment entry option that will lock all related entries for the tagged entry on submit. This includes both entries that are in the tagged entry's list of related entries, and any entry that contains the tagged entry as a related entry.
�As related entries can only be set programmatically, this tag is only expected to be set programmatically, and will likely not function if set manually.
As an example of its use, consider the Index Maker plugin in the ELN. This has two entries; the GUI and the Review Index Assignments entry. The GUI entry is allowed to not lock, so that it can be resubmitted without needing to be unlocked by someone with sufficient privileges - this is useful, as the review entry will only appear once it has been submitted. With the use of this tag, the GUI entry can be locked at the same time as the review entry: on submit of the review entry.
RUN VALIDATION PLUGINS ON LOCK
When this task option value is present, validation plugins will be run for every dependent entry locked.

Description: Modifies field values of attached records of the defined DataType on Entry or Submit. New values for specified fields are defined in the Task Option value.
Tag Help:
Plugins
SetFields [Task: Entry, Task: Submit]
Set field values on the records of the specified data types. The execution may be modified from the default value (ON TASK SUBMIT: MIDDLE) to any other supported execution order. Please refer to the table listed below for supported execution values.�
In addition to allowing specification of static values, this tag provides a number of context specific variables that may be used within the value definition, which will be substituted for the appropriate values. Please refer to the table listed below for the supported context variables.
Example: When executing in a workflow named DNA Extraction, tag value of "In Process - [WorkflowName]" will be converted to "In Process - DNA Extraction"
Supported Executions
Tag Value	Description
ON TASK ENTRY	Execute on entry into the task with the default modifier of MIDDLE
ON TASK ENTRY: FIRST	Execute first on entry into the task
ON TASK ENTRY: EARLY	Execute early on entry into the task
ON TASK ENTRY: MIDDLE	Execute middle on entry into the task
ON TASK ENTRY: LATE	Execute late on entry into the task
ON TASK ENTRY: LAST	Execute last on entry into the task
ON TASK SUBMIT	Execute on submit of the task with a default value of MIDDLE
ON TASK SUBMIT: FIRST	Execute first on submit of the task
ON TASK SUBMIT: EARLY	Execute early on submit of the task
ON TASK SUBMIT: MIDDLE	Execute middle on submit of the task
ON TASK SUBMIT: LATE	Execute middle on submit of the task
ON TASK SUBMIT: LAST	Execute last on submit of the task
Context Variables
Context Variable	Description
[WorkflowName]	Name of the current workflow
[TaskName]	Name of the current task
[ActiveWorkflowId]	Identifier of the current active workflow
[ActiveTaskId]	
Identifier of the current active task
[ExperimentId]	The ExperimentId value of the experiment record associated with the current workflow
[NotebookName]	Name of the current notebook
[EntryName]	Name of the current entry
[NotebookId]	Identifier of the current notebook
[EntryId]	Identifier of the current experiment entry
[Date]	Current date in the format of yyyy/MM/dd. Example: 2016/12/01
[Time]	Current time in the format of HH:mm:ss z. Example: 17:23:34 EST
[User]	User name of the executing user, in the format of Last, First MI. Example: Snow, John K.
Any undefined values (first, last or middle initial) will be truncated from the result. If none of the values are defined, then a user name will be used.
[UserGroup]	Name of the user group that the current user belongs to. If user does not belong to any group, then UNDEFINED will be used.

USE EXISTING LOT OPTION
as option key.
Warning: this tag will have no effect when set on the drawer. It must be set on the ELN plugin entries of "Reactants" or "Products" directly.
The possible option values are:
OVERWRITE_AMOUNT
This option will overwrite the current values with the values from the selected lot, including purity and yield.
NO_AMOUNT_CHANGE
This option will simply link the selected lot without making any changes to the lot's values.
DEDUCT_AMOUNT
This option will deduct the used amount from the lot without changing purity and yield. The remaining amount will be updated accordingly, and it will not allow the remaining amount to go negative.
